name: Ascend Configuration System
description: Two-tier configuration system for Ascend. Manages user configuration stored in ~/.ascend/config.yaml with fallback to hard-coded defaults. Provides functions to load, save, get, and set configuration values with type coercion.

types.name: AscendConfig

fields.name: anthropic_api_key_env
fields.description: Environment variable name for Anthropic API key

fields.name: default_lookback_hours
fields.description: Default time window for analysis in hours

fields.name: linear_api_key_env
fields.description: Environment variable name for Linear API key

fields.name: slack_channels
fields.description: List of Slack channel names to monitor

fields.name: slack_bot_token_env
fields.description: Environment variable name for Slack bot token

types.description: Pydantic model representing the complete Ascend configuration with all settings including directories, API keys, and integration settings.

functions.name: load_config
functions.description: Load configuration from YAML file or return default config if file does not exist. Reads from config_path or defaults to ~/.ascend/config.yaml. Filters out None values from YAML data.

inputs.name: config_path
inputs.description: Custom path to config file, defaults to CONFIG_PATH (~/.ascend/config.yaml)

error_cases.name: yaml_parse_error
error_cases.condition: YAML file is malformed or cannot be parsed

error_cases.name: pydantic_validation_error
error_cases.condition: YAML data contains invalid values for AscendConfig fields

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: File exists but cannot be read due to permissions or IO error

postconditions: If config file exists and is valid YAML, returns config with values from file merged with defaults
If config file does not exist, returns AscendConfig with all default values
side_effects: Reads from filesystem at config_path or CONFIG_PATH

structured_side_effects.description: Reads YAML configuration file if it exists

functions.name: save_config
functions.description: Save AscendConfig to YAML file. Creates parent directories if they don't exist. Writes config as YAML with non-flow style and preserves key order.

inputs.name: config_path
inputs.description: Custom path to config file, defaults to CONFIG_PATH (~/.ascend/config.yaml)

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Cannot write to file due to permissions or IO error

preconditions: cfg must be a valid AscendConfig instance
postconditions: Config file exists at config_path or CONFIG_PATH
Config file contains YAML representation of cfg
Parent directory of config file exists
side_effects: Creates parent directories if they don't exist
Writes to filesystem at config_path or CONFIG_PATH

structured_side_effects.description: Writes configuration to YAML file, creating parent directories as needed

functions.name: get_config_value
functions.description: Get a single configuration value by key. If cfg is not provided, loads config from disk. Returns string representation of the value.

inputs.name: cfg
inputs.description: Optional config object; if None, loads from disk

error_cases.name: key_error
error_cases.condition: key is not a valid field in AscendConfig

error_cases.name: yaml_parse_error
error_cases.condition: Config file exists but YAML is malformed (when cfg is None)

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Cannot read config file (when cfg is None)

preconditions: key must be a valid field name in AscendConfig
postconditions: Returns string representation of the config value for the given key
side_effects: If cfg is None, reads config file from disk via load_config()

structured_side_effects.description: Conditionally reads config file if cfg parameter is None

functions.name: set_config_value
functions.description: Set a single configuration value by key and save to disk. Loads existing config, applies type coercion (int for int fields, comma-separated list for list fields), updates the value, and saves. Returns updated config.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: New value as string; will be coerced to appropriate type

inputs.name: config_path
inputs.description: Custom path to config file, defaults to CONFIG_PATH (~/.ascend/config.yaml)

error_cases.name: key_error
error_cases.condition: key is not a valid field in AscendConfig

error_cases.name: value_error
error_cases.condition: value cannot be converted to required type (e.g., non-numeric string for int field)

error_cases.name: pydantic_validation_error
error_cases.condition: Updated data fails Pydantic validation when constructing new AscendConfig

error_cases.name: yaml_parse_error
error_cases.condition: Existing config file has malformed YAML

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Cannot read or write config file

preconditions: key must be a valid field name in AscendConfig
If field type is int, value must be convertible to int
If field type is list, value should be comma-separated strings
postconditions: Config file contains updated value for key
Value is type-coerced: int for int fields, list for list fields, string otherwise
side_effects: Creates parent directories if they don't exist

invariants: CONFIG_PATH is always ~/.ascend/config.yaml unless overridden by parameter
_DEFAULTS dict provides fallback values for all configurable fields
AscendConfig always has valid default values for all fields
Config values are never None after load_config (None values are filtered out from YAML)
Type coercion in set_config_value: int fields parsed as int, list fields split by comma, others remain string

data_access.rationale: Reads and writes YAML configuration files at ~/.ascend/config.yaml or custom paths. Creates directories as needed.

side_effects.rationale: Reads user configuration from YAML file
Writes updated configuration to YAML file
Creates parent directories for config file if they don't exist

authority.rationale: This module is the authoritative source for all Ascend configuration including paths, API keys environment variable names, model selection, and integration settings. It owns the configuration schema and persistence.
